# Test: Overlay Deletion Recovery (Edge Case)
# Validates that apx sync handles manual overlay deletion gracefully.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create app with dependencies
mkdir app
cd app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/overlay/v1
exists apx.yaml

# Add dependencies
exec apx add proto/common/v1@v1.0.0
exec apx add proto/payments/ledger/v1@v1.0.0
exists apx.lock

# Verify dependencies in apx.yaml
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.yaml
exec grep 'proto/payments/ledger/v1' apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'App with two dependencies'

# Step 2: Generate overlays
exec apx gen go
exists internal/gen/go/proto/common/v1
exists internal/gen/go/proto/payments/ledger/v1

# Step 3: Sync to create go.work
exec apx sync
exists go.work

# Step 4: Manually delete one overlay (simulate accidental deletion)
exec rm -rf internal/gen/go/proto/common/v1

# Verify it's gone
! exists internal/gen/go/proto/common/v1

# Still exists
exists internal/gen/go/proto/payments/ledger/v1

# Step 5: Re-generate should restore the deleted overlay
exec apx gen go
exists internal/gen/go/proto/common/v1
exists internal/gen/go/proto/payments/ledger/v1

# Step 6: Sync should still work
exec apx sync
exists go.work

cd ..

# Success: Overlay deletion recovery validated
# - Manually deleted overlays can be regenerated
# - Remaining overlays are unaffected
# - go.work is maintained after regeneration
